title: RHCSA 10 Mock Exam F
description: A RHCSA 10 mock exam focused on RHEL 10 administration, Flatpak, systemd timers, storage, networking, users, security, and services.

tasks: Set hostname to clientf.exam10.lab and map serverf.exam10.lab to 192.168.122.3.
Configure System eth1 with IPv4 address 192.168.122.65/24, gateway 192.168.122.1, and DNS 192.168.122.3.
Create /var/www/html/f.html and restore its default SELinux context.
Persistently enable httpd_can_network_connect.
Activate the throughput-performance tuned profile.
Create system Flatpak remote examfflatpak pointing to file:///opt/rhcsa/flatpak/repo with GPG verification disabled.
Ensure org.rhcsa.Tools is not installed after configuring examfflatpak.
Configure persistent systemd journal storage.
Allow %teamf10 to run /usr/bin/systemctl without a password by using a sudoers drop-in.
Create group teamf10, create user userf10, set password cinder9, and add the user to teamf10.
Create a cron job for userf10 that writes EXAM10 to /home/userf10/exam10.log every 15 minutes.
Create /usr/local/bin/f-who that prints the primary group for the supplied user argument.
Set the default target to multi-user.target without rebooting.
Create gzip archive /root/f-etc.tar.gz containing /etc/hosts and /etc/fstab.
Install lsof and ensure tcpdump is removed.
Create and enable examftimer.timer that runs every 10 minutes.
Allow TCP port 8105 permanently in firewalld and reload.
Create enabled BaseOS and AppStream repository definitions using http://server/repo/BaseOS/ and http://server/repo/AppStream/ with GPG checks disabled.
Set maximum password age for userf10 to 50 days and warning period to 7 days.
Write users whose shell ends with sh to /root/f-shell-users.txt.
Create /root/f-original, hard link /root/f-hard, and symlink /root/f-soft.
Create VG vgf10 and LV dataf mounted at /mnt/dataf10.
hints: Make changes persistent unless a question says otherwise.
Use local repositories and local documentation; do not depend on internet access.
Flatpak and systemd timer tasks belong to the RHCSA 10 track.
checks: connection_name="System eth1"; nmcli -g NAME connection show "$connection_name" >/dev/null 2>&1 || connection_name="$(private_dev="$(ip -o -4 addr show | awk '$4 ~ /^192\.168\.122\./ {print $2; exit}')"; nmcli -t -f NAME,DEVICE connection show --active | awk -F: -v private_dev="$private_dev" '$2 == private_dev {print $1; exit}')"; test -n "$connection_name" && nmcli -g ipv4.addresses connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx '192.168.122.65/24' && nmcli -g ipv4.gateway connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx '192.168.122.1' && nmcli -g ipv4.dns connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx '192.168.122.3' && nmcli -g ipv4.method connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx manual
getsebool httpd_can_network_connect | grep -Eq -- '--> on$' && semanage boolean -l -C | grep -E '^httpd_can_network_connect\s+\(on\s*,\s*on\)'
getent group teamf10 >/dev/null && id -nG userf10 | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -qx teamf10
systemctl cat examftimer.timer | grep -Fqx 'OnCalendar=*:0/10' && systemctl is-enabled examftimer.timer | grep -qx enabled

awk 'function flush(){if(baseurl ~ /^http:\/\/server\/repo\/BaseOS\/?$/ && enabled == "1" && gpgcheck == "0") base=1;if(baseurl ~ /^http:\/\/server\/repo\/AppStream\/?$/ && enabled == "1" && gpgcheck == "0") app=1}function value(){sub(/^[^=]*=/, ""); gsub(/^[ \t]+|[ \t]+$/, ""); return $0}/^[[:space:]]*\[/ {flush(); baseurl=enabled=gpgcheck=""; next}/^[[:space:]]*baseurl[[:space:]]*=/ {baseurl=value(); next}/^[[:space:]]*enabled[[:space:]]*=/ {enabled=value(); next}/^[[:space:]]*gpgcheck[[:space:]]*=/ {gpgcheck=value(); next}END{flush(); exit !(base && app)}' /etc/yum.repos.d/*.repo

lv_path=/dev/vgf10/dataf; mapper_path=/dev/mapper/vgf10-dataf; mountpoint=/mnt/dataf10; lv_uuid="$(blkid -s UUID -o value "$lv_path")"; lvs "$lv_path" >/dev/null 2>&1 && findmnt -no TARGET "$mountpoint" | grep -qx "$mountpoint" && awk -v dev="$lv_path" -v mapper="$mapper_path" -v uuid="$lv_uuid" -v target="$mountpoint" '$1 !~ /^#/ && $2 == target && $3 == "xfs" && ($1 == dev || $1 == mapper || $1 == "UUID=" uuid || $1 == "/dev/disk/by-uuid/" uuid) {found=1} END {exit !found}' /etc/fstab
solution_outline: Work through each question independently and verify before moving on.

solution_commands: nmcli connection modify "System eth1" ipv4.addresses 192.168.122.65/24 ipv4.gateway 192.168.122.1 ipv4.dns 192.168.122.3 ipv4.method manual connection.autoconnect yes

cat > /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa10-exam.repo <<'EOF'
[rhcsa10-exam-baseos]
name=RHCSA10 Exam BaseOS
baseurl=http://server/repo/BaseOS/
enabled=1
gpgcheck=0

[rhcsa10-exam-appstream]
name=RHCSA10 Exam AppStream
baseurl=http://server/repo/AppStream/
enabled=1
gpgcheck=0
EOF
